* feat(#1191): address review: fix glob expansion, lint exit gating, test paths

- Replace bash glob `$spec_dir/**/*.md` with `find`-based `md_files`
  array for reliable nested file discovery without globstar
- Track `last_lint_exit` code and gate success on both `lint_exit == 0`
  AND `violation_count == 0` to prevent silent pass when lint fails
  with unparseable output

  empty (unexpected output format)

* fix(#1191): make parse_markdownlint_output explicitly return 0

Add explicit `else :` no-op for unmatched lines and `return 0` after
the pipeline to prevent set -euo pipefail from propagating a non-zero
exit status through the caller.

* fix(#1191): reorder failure-reason branches to prevent misclassification

Move unparseable-output check (last_lint_exit!=0 && violation_count==0)
before the max-iterations-exhausted check. When MARKDOWNLINT_MAX_ITERATIONS=1
and the loop breaks on the last iteration due to unparseable output, the
exhaustion branch no longer fires first. Also tighten the exhaustion guard
to require final_violation_count > 0.

* fix(#1191): validate MARKDOWNLINT_MAX_ITERATIONS as positive base-10 integer

Validate MARKDOWNLINT_MAX_ITERATIONS using the same pattern as
AGDT_PLAN_CONTEXT_BUDGET: check ^[0-9]+$ and 10#value > 0 to avoid
bash arithmetic errors on non-numeric or octal-looking values.
Falls back to default (5) with a warning on invalid input.

* fix(#1191): replace wc -l with awk for whitespace-safe line counts

Replace all `wc -l` usages with `awk 'END{print NR}'` in both
generate-spec-from-issue.sh and the test script. This avoids
leading-whitespace padding from some wc implementations that
can break numeric comparisons and string equality assertions.

* feat(permissions): implement fine-grained permission control (#2145)

* refactor(permission): rename permission check functions for clarity

- User.CanWrite() → User.CanCreateFilesOrFolders()
- common.CanWrite() → common.CanWriteContentBypassUserPerms()
- common.IsApply() → common.MetaCoversPath()

Improves code readability by making function names more descriptive.
The new MetaCoversPath name clearly indicates it checks if a meta rule
covers a specific path. It better conveys that it's a query function
rather than an action, and the applyToSubFolder parameter is more
explicit than applySub.

Also adds comprehensive test coverage:
- 10 tests for MetaCoversPath core logic
- 6 tests for CanWriteContent
UserPerms
- 7 tests for getReadme
- 5 tests for getHeader
- 6 tests for isEncrypt
- 9 tests for whetherHide

Total: 43 test scenarios covering all path matching and permission
inheritance logic. Tests verify both normal behavior and bug fixes
for Readme/Header information leakage and write permission bypass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(permission): implement fine-grained user permissions for read/write operations

Add per-user read and write permission controls at the meta level to enable
more granular access control beyond the existing permission flags.

Key changes:
- Add ReadUsers/WriteUsers fields to Meta model with sub-directory inheritance flags
- Implement CanRead and CanWrite permission check functions in server/common
- Filter file list results based on user read permissions
- Add permission checks across all file operations (FTP, HTTP handlers, WebDAV)
- Simplify error handling pattern for MetaNotFound errors throughout codebase

This allows administrators to restrict specific users from accessing or modifying
certain paths, providing finer control over file system permissions.

Note: Batch and recursive operations (FsMove, FsCopy, FsRemove, FsRecursiveMove,
FsBatchRename, FsRegexRename) currently check parent directory permissions only.
Individual item permission checks are not performed for performance reasons.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* test(permission): add comprehensive tests for CanRead, CanWrite, and combined permission checks

Add TestCanRead, TestCanWrite, TestCanAccessWithReadPermissions, and
TestWritePermissionCombinations to validate the three-layer permission
system including nil user/meta, sub-path inheritance, user whitelists,
and root-level restrictions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(webdav): use safe type assertion for MetaPassKey to prevent panic

Bearer-token and OPTIONS auth paths do not set MetaPassKey in context,
causing a panic when handlers perform a forced type assertion on nil.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(permission): treat nil user as system context in CanRead/CanWrite

Previously, CanRead/CanWrite returned false for nil user, causing
filterReadableObjs to return an empty list when fs.List is called from
internal contexts without a user (e.g. context.Background()). A nil user
represents an internal/system call and should bypass per-user restrictions,
consistent with how whetherHide already handles nil user.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(fsmanage): prevent path traversal in FsRemove

The previous check only skipped names that resolved to "/", but did not
prevent traversal to sibling directories (e.g. "../secret"), which could
bypass the CanWrite permission check that is only applied to req.Dir.

Replace with a post-join prefix check to ensure each resolved path stays
within reqPath.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(webdav): align MetaPassKey behavior with FTP auth logic

For guest users, the WebDAV password input serves as the meta folder
password (consistent with FTP anonymous/guest handling). For authenticated
users, MetaPassKey is set to empty string since their login password is
not the meta folder password.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(permission): require write auth for fs list refresh

* refactor(permission): use MetaCoversPath in CanRead/CanWrite for consistency

Replace inline `(Sub || meta.Path == path)` logic with MetaCoversPath,
consistent with CanWriteContentBypassUserPerms. Also fix a copy-paste
error in the CanWrite comment (read → write).

fix(tests): wait for real worker to be working in concurrency spec to prevent job starvation (#965)

Instead of sleeping 0.5s (hoping the container worker dequeues j1), poll
BawWorkers::ResqueApi.statuses for a working job. This is more reliable
and correctly models waiting for the real container worker rather than
emulating an extra worker.

Fixes #964

docs: add parallel FFmpeg ABR HLS guide and hook scripts

Add documentation and automation for multi-instance adaptive bitrate
HLS encoding using parallel FFmpeg subscribers.

New files:
- scripts/on-publish-abr.sh  — Bash hook: 3 parallel transcoders
- scripts/on-publish-abr.ps1 — PowerShell equivalent

Key design decisions from review:
- Use -force_key_frames (time-based) instead of -g (frame-count)
  for fps-independent segment alignment
- Add -maxrate/-bufsize (VBV) for accurate BANDWIDTH reporting
- Clean stale segments on republish to prevent playlist corruption
- Remove append_list flag to avoid stale playlist accumulation
- Write master.m3u8 without BOM on Windows for parser compatibility
- Add #EXT-X-INDEPENDENT-SEGMENTS to master playlist

Documentation:
- hls-streaming.md: new Parallel FFmpeg ABR section with architecture
  diagram, alignment requirements, step-by-step, comparison table
- scripts/README.md: on-publish-abr entry and directory listing

E3: Pod restart detection via get_status health check

- Add Role::Unknown variant (#[repr(u8)] + TryFrom<u8>) to distinguish
  freshly started pods from explicitly demoted ones (Role::None)
- Add ReplicaStatusInfo struct and get_status() to ReplicaHandle trait
- GrpcReplicaHandle: calls existing GetStatus RPC, updates cached progress
- InProcessReplicaHandle: Mutex<Role> for type-safe role tracking
- Proto: ROLE_UNKNOWN=0 (default), ROLE_NONE=4

Reconciler Healthy phase now probes ALL replicas via get_status():
- Epoch mismatch or Role::Unknown -> stale (pod restarted)
- gRPC error -> stale (pod dead)
- Primary stale -> FailingOver (with stale secondaries cleaned first)
- Secondary stale -> remove handle, requeue for scale-up re-add
- Runs before switchover check (prevents switchover to dead target)

KvClusterApi mock: crash_pod() preserves data_dir (PVC simulation),
restart_pod() reuses it (PVC re-attach). Tests upgraded to crash_pod.

commit.message:
[#10700] test: Harden retention count assertions in TestFunctionMetaService and TestFilesetMetaService (#10701)

### What changes were proposed in this pull request?

This PR hardens the retention cleanup assertions in:

- `TestFunctionMetaService.testDeleteFunctionVersionsByRetentionCount()`
- `TestFilesetMetaService.testDeleteFilesetVersionsByRetentionCount()`

Instead of only checking aggregate active/deleted counts, the tests now:

- verify that all expected versions are active before retention cleanup
- verify exactly which versions are soft deleted after cleanup
- verify that the latest retained versions remain active

This is a test-only change. No product logic is modified.

### Why are the changes needed?

The previous assertions were brittle and low-signal for intermittent CI
failures.

When a failure happened, the tests only reported that the number of
active or deleted versions
did not match the expected count, but they did not reveal which version
state was wrong or whether
the inconsistency was already present before retention cleanup started.

By asserting concrete version states, future failures become easier to
diagnose and the tests more
accurately capture the intended behavior.

Fix: #10700

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

No.

### How was this patch tested?

- `./gradlew --no-daemon :core:spotlessApply :core:test --tests
org.apache.gravitino.storage.relational.service.TestFunctionMetaService.testDeleteFunctionVersionsByRetentionCount
--tests
org.apache.gravitino.storage.relational.service.TestFilesetMetaService.testDeleteFilesetVersionsByRetentionCount
-PskipITs -PskipDockerTests=false`
- Repeated the same targeted test command 3 times locally in embedded
mode

commit.message:
Runner error reclassification, pipeline gap telemetry, and tests (#68)

* reclassify runner-origin errors as TestStatus.Error and report via telemetry

- Add IsRunnerBug flag to TestResult to distinguish runner limitations
  (MockCodeunitHandle/MockRecordHandle InvalidOperationException) from
  genuine test failures (AlDialog.Error, MockAssert)
- Add IsRunnerError() helper in Executor: InvalidOperationException with
  AlRunner.Runtime.Mock frames → TestStatus.Error + IsRunnerBug=true
- Update PrintResults: runner bugs print a GitHub issue link hint instead
  of 'Inject via AL interface' (which is only for NotSupportedException)
- Add TryReportTestErrorsAsync to TelemetryReporter: after each run,
  groups runner-bug errors, shows one prompt, sends them to App Insights
- Refactor GetVersionString/GetOsString to shared helpers
- Wire TryReportTestErrorsAsync in Program.cs after pipeline.Run()

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* unify pipeline gap telemetry: Roslyn exclusions + runtime errors in one prompt

Previously only runtime runner-bug errors were reported. Now ALL pipeline gaps
feed into one telemetry batch:

- Roslyn compilation exclusions (rewriter misses): when the BC-generated C#
  cannot compile after rewriting, the excluded file's Roslyn errors are sent
  as AlRunner.CompilationGap events. This surfaces rewriter gaps so they can
  be fixed proactively.
- Runtime gaps (AlRunner.RuntimeGap): InvalidOperationException from
  AlRunner.Runtime.Mock* — same as before but now deduplicated by message
  before sending (one event per unique error, not one per test).
- Single combined prompt shown once after each run covering both categories.
  No prompt if there are no gaps.

Also adds PrintCompilationGaps() to Executor: prints a WARN block to stdout
after test results listing excluded files with their Roslyn errors and a link
to file an issue. This replaces the quiet INFO log that previously went to
stderr, making rewriter gaps visible to the user.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* add tests for runner-error classification and isRunnerBug JSON field

Tests:
- MockCodeunitHandle_UnknownId_ThrowsClassifiableException: verifies the
  detection component — MockCodeunitHandle(99999).Invoke throws
  InvalidOperationException with 'not found in assembly' and
  'AlRunner.Runtime.Mock' in the stack trace, which is exactly what
  Executor.IsRunnerError() checks.
- PipelineResult_AssertFail_IsRunnerBugFalse: regression guard — user
  assertion failures (test-06) must NOT be classified as runner bugs.
- PipelineResult_TestPass_IsRunnerBugFalse: passing tests must have
  IsRunnerBug = false.
- JsonOutput_IsRunnerBugTrue_AppearsInJson: isRunnerBug:true appears in
  JSON for runner-limitation errors; absent (null) for pass/fail so the
  JSON stays clean for tooling consumers.
- PrintResults_IsRunnerBugTrue_OutputContainsHint: the 'Runner limitation'
  hint with the GitHub issues link appears in stdout for runner-bug errors.

Note: full end-to-end IsRunnerBug=true via the pipeline requires a
package-based scenario (codeunit available as symbol but not compiled into
the assembly). The detection component is covered by the direct Mock test.

Also adds isRunnerBug to SerializeJsonOutput — previously the field was
not included in --output-json output, making it invisible to tooling.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix two review issues from PR #68

1. PrintCompilationGaps: route all output to stderr instead of stdout
   to avoid corrupting JSON when --output-json is active.

2. TelemetryReporter: apply ScrubMessage to each Roslyn error string
   in gapMessages before sending to App Insights. Roslyn diagnostics

fix: handle non-JSON error bodies in EnsureResponseAsync (#244)

When Keycloak sits behind a reverse proxy, errors like 502 Bad Gateway
can return an HTML error page rather than a JSON response. Previously,
EnsureResponseAsync would let JsonSerializer.Deserialize throw a raw
JsonException instead of the expected KeycloakHttpClientException.

This patch wraps the deserialization in a try/catch. If the body is not
valid JSON, the raw text is surfaced as ErrorDescription (with the HTTP
status code as Error) so callers always receive a consistent
KeycloakHttpClientException regardless of the error body format.

Adds HttpResponseExtensionsTests covering:
- Valid JSON error body → KeycloakHttpClientException with parsed fields
- Non-JSON (HTML) body → KeycloakHttpClientException, no JsonException
- Empty / whitespace body → KeycloakHttpClientException with defaults
- Successful response → no exception

Fix VLAN socket binding to secondary IP on multi-IP interfaces (#99)

In prepare_vlan_sockets(), the code iterates getifaddrs() and overwrites
client_addr with each IPv4 address found on the VLAN interface, ending
up with the last one. On interfaces with a secondary IP (e.g. Vlan1000
with 192.168.0.1/21 primary and 192.169.0.1/22 secondary), the socket
gets bound to the secondary IP, causing all client-facing DHCP responses
to use the wrong source address.

Fix: use addr_is_primary() (introduced in the parent commit) to skip
secondary IPs and break on the first primary IP found.

Signed-off-by: Xichen Lin <lukelin0907@gmail.com>

commit.message:
[#10743] fix(mcp): pin fakeredis below 2.35.0 for FastMCP compatibility (#10744)

### What changes were proposed in this pull request?

This PR fixes an MCP runtime compatibility issue by pinning `fakeredis`
to a version range compatible with transitive dependency `pydocket`.

- Added `fakeredis<2.35.0` in `mcp-server/pyproject.toml`
- Regenerated `mcp-server/uv.lock`

### Why are the changes needed?

`fakeredis` 2.35.0 removed `FakeConnection` from `fakeredis.aioredis`,
while `pydocket` (used transitively by `fastmcp`) still imports it at
runtime.
That causes MCP client connection failures with errors like:
`cannot import name 'FakeConnection' from 'fakeredis.aioredis'`.

Fix: #10743

### Does this PR introduce _any_ user-facing change?

Yes. MCP server/client startup becomes stable again under the current
dependency set and no longer fails on `FakeConnection` import errors.

### How was this patch tested?

- `uv lock`
- `uv run pytest tests/unit/tools/test_catalog.py -q`

perf: token cost optimization (6 tasks, 3 waves) (#15)

* docs: add token cost optimization plan v2

6-task plan organized into 3 workstreams and 3 gated rollout waves.
Reviewed by 3 independent PE models (GPT 5.4, Claude Opus 4.6, Goldeneye).
2 tasks killed (slim prompt, skip SDK validation). Sequential waterfall
replaces additive savings model.

Closes #7 #9 #10 #11 #12 #13 (plan only, implementation tracked per-wave)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: add compact return summaries to all pipeline agents (#9)

Each agent now returns a structured JSON summary (schema v1) as its
final message instead of prose. Includes schema_version and warnings[]
for forward compatibility. Reduces orchestrator context accumulation
by ~50K tokens per pipeline run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: use canonical checklist reference in audit agents (#12)

Eliminates redundant cli-audit skill invocations and repeated file
reads. Auditors now read quality-checklist.md directly once at start
instead of invoking the skill. Single source of truth maintained.
Return summaries also added for both auditors.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: add parallel resource dispatch to CLI generator (#11)

For CLIs with >5 resource groups, the generator now spawns per-resource
subagents in batches of 4. Shared modules (client, auth, types, errors)
are still built sequentially first. Caps per-subagent context at ~30K
tokens vs 120K+ for the monolithic approach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

* perf: add immutable per-phase output artifacts (#13)

Orchestrator writes compact, immutable output manifests after each
phase (.cli-pipeline/phase-outputs/phase-NN-*.json). Later phases
read these ~200-500 token summaries instead of re-parsing large
artifacts. Schema-versioned for forward compatibility.

fix(coc): hardcode ws:// protocol for WebSocket connections

Add getWsUrl() utility in config.ts that returns the full ws:// base
URL. All 7 WebSocket hooks now use this shared helper instead of
duplicating protocol detection logic.

The CoC server only listens on HTTP, so WSS connections through
reverse proxies (e.g. Azure Dev Tunnels) fail because the server
cannot negotiate TLS. Always use ws:// since the server is plain HTTP.

commit.message:
fix: guarantee prebuffer EOF sentinel and abort on prebuffer error

1. EOF sentinel (None) was silently dropped when the prebuffer queue
   was full — put_nowait(None) suppressed by QueueFull. Now makes room
   by discarding one data chunk before placing the sentinel, preventing
   _yield_from_prebuffer() from blocking forever.

2. After draining a failed prebuffer, get_audio_stream fell through to
   _signal_track_completion() — treating a CDN/HTTP error as natural
   track end, desyncing Ynison queue state. Now checks prebuffer.error
   after the yield loop and aborts instead of signaling completion.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
